questions.question: Which term is used to define the field of chemistry which is related to linking molecules like those in living cells?
questions.explanation: The Nobel Prize in Chemistry has been jointly awarded to Carolyn Bertozzi, Morton Meldal and Barry Sharpless for their work on snipping molecules together. Also known as ‘Click chemistry’, it is related to the process of linking together molecules like those in living cells. Their work is used to explore cells, track biological processes and can be applied in cancer treatment drugs.

questions.question: As per the recent study, which region is the world’s fastest-growing crypto adopter?
questions.explanation: As per the recent report of blockchain researcher Chainalysis, the Middle East and North Africa are the world’s fastest-growing cryptocurrency markets. While the MENA region is one of the smallest crypto markets, it has grown rapidly to USD 566 billion between July 2021 and June 2022. The volume of crypto received in the region jumped 48% in the year to June.

questions.question: Nalagarh, where a 350-crore Medical Device Park is set to be built, is located in which state?
questions.explanation: Prime Minister (PM) Narendra Modi laid the foundation stone of the Medical Device Park at Nalagarh, Himachal Pradesh (HP) The park is to be built at a cost of about Rs 350 crore. Himachal Pradesh is one of the three states that have been selected for the Bulk Drugs Park. Himachal Pradesh is also one of the four states that have been selected for Medical Devices Park and the Nalagarh Medical Device Park is part of this.

questions.question: Which Indian player has been named the FIH Player of the Year in the men’s category in 2022?
questions.explanation: India ace Hockey Player Harmanpreet Singh was named the FIH Player of the Year in the men’s category for the second successive time. He became only the fourth player to win the Player of the Year award (men’s category) in consecutive years. Albers of Netherlands have been named the FIH Player of the Year 2021-22 in the women’s category. Harmanpreet scored 18 goals from 16 games, with two hat-tricks, in the FIH Hockey Pro League 2021-’22.

questions.question: Which institution launched the ‘Education 4.0 India Report’?
questions.explanation: As part of its Education 4.0 India initiative, the World Economic Forum has launched a new ‘Education 4.0 India Report’. The report deliberates on how digital and other technologies can address learning gaps and make education accessible to all. The report is the result of collaboration between the World Economic Forum, UNICEF and YuWaah.

questions.question: Which country detected the Interstellar meteors IM1 and IM2?
questions.explanation: According to new research by Harvard University astrophysicists, both the first and second interstellar meteors, IM1 and IM2, are outliers in material strength and may have originated in supernova explosions. IM1 was detected by U.S. government sensors in 2014, identified as interstellar object candidate in 2019, and was confirmed in 2022. The second interstellar meteor, IM2 was identified in data from the NASA’s Center for NEO Studies (CNEOS) fireball catalog.

questions.question: What is the theme of the ‘World Post Day 2022’?
questions.explanation: The World Post Day is celebrated every year on October 9 across the globe to create awareness on the role of the postal sector in daily life. This year’s theme for the day is ‘Post for Planet’. It is also the anniversary of the founding of the Universal Postal Union in Bern, Switzerland, in 1874. With more than 1.5 lakh post offices, India Post is the largest postal network in the world.

questions.question: Which company’s pilot project on Flexi-Fuel Strong Hybrid Electric Vehicles (FFV-SHEV) was launched in India recently?
questions.explanation: Union Minister for Road Transport and Highways Nitin Gadkari launched Toyota’s first of its kind pilot project on Flexi-Fuel Strong Hybrid Electric Vehicles (FFV-SHEV) in India. The vehicles are said to run on 100 per cent petrol as well as 20 to 100 per cent blended ethanol and electric power.

questions.question: Which Indian state is the host of the FIFA U-17 Women’s World Cup?
questions.explanation: Odisha’s Kalinga Stadium, located in the centre of Bhubaneswar, is set to host the FIFA U-17 Women’s World Cup. Kalinga Stadium, named after the state’s ancient name Kalinga, will also host the Men’s Hockey World Cup consecutively for the second time beginning from January 2023. Chief Minister Naveen Patnaik launched ‘Football for All’ an initiative under which over 43,000 footballs will be distributed in schools across Odisha to popularise the game.

questions.question: Which state/UT inaugurated CESL Solar-powered charging plaza for electric vehicles?
questions.explanation: Convergence Energy Services Ltd (CESL) is a wholly owned subsidiary of Energy Efficiency Services Ltd (EESL) under the Ministry of Power. CESL has established a first-of-its-kind solar-powered charging plaza for electric vehicles in Leh, in the Ladakh region. The carport can be used to charge electric buses as well. Customised electric cars, made available by CES and manufactured by Hyundai Motor, were added in official fleet of Leh administration.
